revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Takaku Shrine |image =
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|19|20|N|139|19|28.5|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|20.0|N|139|19|28.5|E|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) }} [[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]] '''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} (former district shrine). The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=}}.Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc. == History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma). The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=}}.[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town) The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine. The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]]. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之41村里部淘綾郡巻之3|chapter=二ノ宮庄 高麗寺村 高麗権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/167}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese) {{神奈川県の神社}} {{郷社}} {{高麗神社}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]] ]]]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|高来神社|version=103869249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Goguryeo refugees]] [[Category:Shinbutsu-shūgō]] [[Category:High-Ranking shrines]] [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:Takaku Shrine in 2002.jpg|260px]] [[ja:高来神社]] [[ja:鶏足山高麗寺大権現]] [[en:Takaku Shrine]] [[sn:akaku Shrine]] [[sn:Takaku Shrine]] [[Category:大磯町の建築物]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:淘綾郡]] [[Category:渡来人]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]]

Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之41村里部淘綾郡巻之3|chapter=二ノ宮庄 高麗寺村 高麗権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/167}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese) {{神奈川県の神社}} {{郷社}} {{高麗神社}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]] ]]]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|高来神社|version=103869249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Goguryeo refugees]] [[Category:Shinbutsu-shūgō]] [[Category:High-Ranking shrines]] [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:Takaku Shrine in 2002.jpg|260px]] [[ja:高来神社]] [[ja:鶏足山高麗寺大権現]] [[en:Takaku Shrine]] [[sn:akaku Shrine]] [[sn:Takaku Shrine]] [[Category:大磯町の建築物]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:淘綾郡]] {{Shrine |name = Takaku Shrine |image =
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|19|20|N|139|19|28.5|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|20.0|N|139|19|28.5|E|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) }} [[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]] '''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} (former district shrine). The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=}}.Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc. == History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma). The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=}}.[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town) The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine. The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]]. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之41村里部淘綾郡巻之3|chapter=二ノ宮庄 高麗寺村 高麗権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/167}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese) {{神奈川県の神社}} {{郷社}} {{高麗神社}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]] ]]]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|高来神社|version=103869249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Goguryeo refugees]] [[Category:Shinbutsu-shūgō]] [[Category:High-Ranking shrines]] [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:Takaku Shrine in 2002.jpg|260px]] [[ja:高来神社]] [[ja:鶏足山高麗寺大権現]] [[sn:akaku Shrine]] [[sn:Takaku Shrine]] [[Category:大磯町の建築物]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:淘綾郡]] [[Category:渡来人]] {{Shrine |name = Takaku Shrine |image =
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|19|20|N|139|19|28.5|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|20.0|N|139|19|28.5|E|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) }} [[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]] '''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (former district shrine). The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc. == History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma). The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town) The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine. The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]]. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之41村里部淘綾郡巻之3|chapter=二ノ宮庄 高麗寺村 高麗権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/167}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese) {{神奈川県の神社}} {{郷社}} {{高麗神社}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]] ]]]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|高来神社|version=103869249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Goguryeo refugees]] [[Category:Shinbutsu-shūgō]] [[Category:High-Ranking shrines]] [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:Takaku Shrine in 2002.jpg|260px]] [[ja:高来神社]] [[ja:鶏足山高麗寺大権現]] [[sn:akaku Shrine]] [[sn:Takaku Shrine]] [[Category:大磯町の建築物]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:淘綾郡]] [[Category:渡来人]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之41村里部淘綾郡巻之3|chapter=二ノ宮庄 高麗寺村 高麗権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/167}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * {{Shrine |name = Takaku Shrine |image =
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|19|20|N|139|19|28.5|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[:en:〒|〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|20.0|N|139|19|28.5|E|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) }} [[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]] '''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (former district shrine). The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc. == History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma). The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town) The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine. The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]]. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之41村里部淘綾郡巻之3|chapter=二ノ宮庄 高麗寺村 高麗権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/167}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese) {{神奈川県の神社}} {{郷社}} {{高麗神社}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]] ]]]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|高来神社|version=103869249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu-shūgō]] [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:Takaku Shrine in 2002.jpg|260px]] [[ja:高来神社]] [[ja:鶏足山高麗寺大権現]] [[sn:akaku Shrine]] [[sn:Takaku Shrine]] [[Category:大磯町の建築物]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:淘綾郡]] [[Category:渡来人]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Takaku Shrine |image =
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|19|20|N|139|19|28.5|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[:en:〒|〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|20.0|N|139|19|28.5|E|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) }} [[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]] '''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (former district shrine). The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc. == History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma). The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town) The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine. The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]]. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之41村里部淘綾郡巻之3|chapter=二ノ宮庄 高麗寺村 高麗権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/167}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese) {{神奈川県の神社}} {{郷社}} {{高麗神社}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]] ]]]] [[Category:Orphaned translated pages]] {{translated page|ja|高来神社|version=103869249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:Takaku Shrine in 2002.jpg|260px]] [[ja:高来神社]] [[ja:鶏足山高麗寺大権現]] [[sn:akaku Shrine]] [[sn:Takaku Shrine]] [[Category:大磯町の建築物]] [[Category:大磯町の歴史]] [[Category:淘綾郡]] [[Category:渡来人]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Koma clan]] {{Commons category|Takaku-jinja (Oiso)}}

The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town) The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine. The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]]. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之41村里部淘綾郡巻之3|chapter=二ノ宮庄 高麗寺村 高麗権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/167}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (former district shrine). The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc. == History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma). The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town) The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine. The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]]. == Enshrined Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite revision.comment: Bot: remove obsolete category Orphaned translated pages
revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Takaku Shrine |image =
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|19|20|N|139|19|28.5|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[:en:〒|〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|20.0|N|139|19|28.5|E|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) }} [[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]] '''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (former district shrine). The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc. == History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma). The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town) The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine. The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]]. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之41村里部淘綾郡巻之3|chapter=二ノ宮庄 高麗寺村 高麗権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/167}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese) {{神奈川県の神社}} {{郷社}} {{高麗神社}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]] ]]]] {{translated page|ja|高来神社|version=103869249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:Takaku Shrine in 2002.jpg|260px]] [[ja:高来神社]] [[ja:鶏足山高麗寺大権現]] [[sn:akaku Shrine]] [[sn:Takaku Shrine]] [[Category:大磯町の建築物]] [[Category:大磯の Municipal History]] [[Category:淘綾郡]] [[Category:Toraijin]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] {{Shrine |name = Takaku Shrine |image =
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|19|20|N|139|19|28.5|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[:en:〒|〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|20.0|N|139|19|28.5|E|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) }} [[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]] '''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (former district shrine). The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc. == History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma). The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town) The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine. The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]]. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之41村里部淘綾郡巻之3|chapter=二ノ宮庄 高麗寺村 高麗権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/167}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|qq=}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese) {{神奈川県の神社}} {{郷社}} {{高麗神社}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]] ]]]] {{translated page|ja|高来神社|version=103869249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Gongen]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[image:Takaku Shrine in 2002.jpg|260px]] [[ja:高来神社]] During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine. The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]]. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11670255]] Wikidata name: Takaku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takaku Shrine |image =
Haiden (Worship Hall)
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=260|coord={{coord|35|19|20|N|139|19|28.5|E}}}} |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[:en:〒|〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|19|20.0|N|139|19|28.5|E|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) }} [[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]] '''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (former district shrine). The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}.Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc. == History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]. The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma). The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town) The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine. The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]]. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] == Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings == Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years == Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot). == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite
