[[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]]
'''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (former district shrine).
The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}.<ref>Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc.</ref>
== History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].
The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=|1=Mount Koma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10872479}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma).
The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=|1=Koma no Jakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8010424}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town)</ref>
The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.
The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=|1=Koma Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673304}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]].
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takaku-jinja (Oiso)]][[ja:高来神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11670255]] Wikidata name: Takaku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takaku Shrine |image = File:Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa- 2023 April 16 various 22 39 54 464000.jpeg |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[:en:〒|〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.322222|N|139.324583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) |location = {{ill|Oiso|qid=Q385023}} |deity = {{ill|Kamimusubi|qid=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Ninigi|qid=Q1056017}} }}
[[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]]
'''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (former district shrine).
The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}.<ref>Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc.</ref>
== History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].
The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=|1=Mount Koma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10872479}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma).
The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=|1=Koma no Jakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8010424}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town)</ref>
The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.
The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=|1=Koma Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673304}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]].
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years

== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takaku-jinja (Oiso)]][[ja:高来神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11670255]] Wikidata name: Takaku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takaku Shrine |image = File:Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa- 2023 April 16 various 22 39 54 464000.jpeg |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[:en:〒|〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.322222|N|139.324583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) |location = {{ill|Oiso|WD=Q385023}} |deity = {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017}} }}
[[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]]
'''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (former district shrine).
The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}.<ref>Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc.</ref>
== History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].
The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|qq=|1=Mount Koma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10872479}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma).
The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|qq=|1=Koma no Jakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8010424}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town)</ref>
The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.
The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|qq=|1=Koma Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673304}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]].
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years

== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takaku-jinja (Oiso)]][[ja:高来神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11670255]] Wikidata name: Takaku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takaku Shrine |image = File:Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa- 2023 April 16 various 22 39 54 464000.jpeg |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[:en:〒|〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.322222|N|139.324583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) |location = {{ill|Oiso|WD=Q385023}} |deity = {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017}} }}
[[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]]
'''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (former district shrine).
The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}.<ref>Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc.</ref>
== History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].
The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|1=Mount Koma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10872479}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma).
The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|1=Koma no Jakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8010424}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town)</ref>
The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.
The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|1=Koma Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673304}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]].
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years

== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot).
== References == {{Reflist}}

== Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|1=Koma district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673318}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takaku-jinja (Oiso)]][[ja:高来神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11670255]] Wikidata name: Takaku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takaku Shrine |image = File:Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa- 2023 April 16 various 22 39 54 464000.jpeg |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[:en:〒|〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.322222|N|139.324583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) |location = {{ill|Oiso|WD=Q385023}} |deity = {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017}} }}
[[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]]
'''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (former district shrine).
The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}.<ref>Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc.</ref>
== History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].
The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|1=Mount Koma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10872479}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma).
The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|1=Koma no Jakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8010424}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town)</ref>
The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.
The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|1=Koma Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673304}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]].
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot).

== External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese)
{{神奈川県の神社}} {{郷社}} {{高麗神社}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takaku-jinja (Oiso)]][[ja:高来神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11670255]] Wikidata name: Takaku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takaku Shrine |image = File:Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa- 2023 April 16 various 22 39 54 464000.jpeg |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[:en:〒|〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.322222|N|139.324583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) |location = {{ill|Oiso|WD=Q385023}} |deity = {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017}} }}
[[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]]
'''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (former district shrine).
The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}.<ref>Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc.</ref>
== History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].
The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|1=Mount Koma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10872479}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma).
The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|1=Koma no Jakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8010424}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town)</ref>
The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.
The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|1=Koma Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673304}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]].
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot).

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takaku-jinja (Oiso)]][[ja:高来神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11670255]] Wikidata name: Takaku Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Takaku Shrine |image = File:Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa- 2023 April 16 various 22 39 54 464000.jpeg |location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]][[:en:Naka District, Kanagawa|Naka District, Kanagawa]][[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]] Koma 2-chome 9-47 ([[:en:〒|〒]]255-0001) |coordinates = {{Coord|35.322222|N|139.324583|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deities = {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}}, {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]] |shrine_Rank = {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |established = During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] |nickname = Takaku-san (Komasan) |location = {{ill|Oiso|WD=Q385023}} |deity = {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Ninigi|WD=Q1056017}} }}
[[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]]
'''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (former district shrine).
The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}.<ref>Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc.</ref>
== History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].
The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|1=Mount Koma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10872479}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma).
The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|1=Koma no Jakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8010424}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town)</ref>
The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.
The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|1=Koma Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673304}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]].
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot).
== References == {{Reflist}}

== Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|1=Koma district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673318}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese)
{{神奈川県の神社}} {{郷社}} {{高麗神社}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]

'''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (former district shrine).
The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}.<ref>Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc.</ref>
== History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].
The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|1=Mount Koma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10872479}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma).
The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|1=Koma no Jakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8010424}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town)</ref>
The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.
The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|1=Koma Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673304}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]].
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot).

'''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (former district shrine).
The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}.<ref>Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc.</ref>
== History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].
The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|1=Mount Koma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10872479}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma).
The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|1=Koma no Jakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8010424}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town)</ref>
The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.
The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|1=Koma Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673304}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]].
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot).
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之41村里部淘綾郡巻之3|chapter=二ノ宮庄 高麗寺村 高麗権現社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/167}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Koma Shrine|Koma Shrine]] * {{ill|Koma District (Musashi Province)|ja|高麗郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District (Musashi Province)|1=Koma district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673318}} * [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * [[:en:Koma clan|Koma clan]] * {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=802&cd=1209245&scd=&npg=0 Takaku Shrine - Kanagawa Shrine Association] (Japanese) * [http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/look/jisya/takakujinja.html Takaku Shrine - Ōiso Town Tourism Guide] (Japanese)
{{神奈川県の神社}} {{郷社}} {{高麗神社}} ]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]]
]]]]

To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|1=Koma no Jakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8010424}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town)</ref>

During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.

The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.
The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|1=Koma Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673304}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]].
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot).

[[File:Takaku shrine - Oiso Kanagawa - 2023 June 3 various 16 04 25 116000.jpeg|thumb|250px|Torii and approach path (2023 June)]]
'''Takaku Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高来神社}}, also known as '''Koma Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗神社}}) is a Shinto shrine located in [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]], Japan. The shrine holds the status of an {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (former district shrine).
The name "Takaku" is said to originate from the arrival of settlers from [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]], an ancient kingdom on the {{ill|Korean Peninsula|ja|朝鮮半島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Korean Peninsula|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Korean Peninsula|WD=Q483134}}.<ref>Pronunciation from [[:en:Wu Chinese|Wu Chinese]], [[:en:Southern Min|Southern Min]], and [[:en:Cantonese|Cantonese]]: kau-le, kau-lâi (ko-lâi), ko-lòi, etc.</ref>
== History == The exact founding date is unknown, but records claim it was established during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]].
The shrine was once located at the summit of {{ill|Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|ja|高麗山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Koma (Kanagawa)|1=Mount Koma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10872479}}, where it was known as '''Koma Gongen Shrine''' ({{lang|ja|高麗権現社}}). To its right was {{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}, and to its left stood [[:en:Vaiśravaṇa|Bishamonten]]. Together, these were known as the '''Koma Sansha Gongen''' (Three Shrines of Koma).
The shrine is closely linked to the migration of [[:en:Goguryeo|Goguryeo]] people led by {{ill|Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|ja|高麗若光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Jakko (Koma Wakaikō)|1=Koma no Jakkō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8010424}}, who settled in [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] after the fall of their kingdom. The shrine's festival, known as "Mifune Matsuri" (Boat Festival), includes a song referencing the "Protector of Koma" who became the guardian of {{ill|Ōiso Bay|ja|大磯浦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōiso Bay|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref>[http://www.town.oiso.kanagawa.jp/isotabi/taiken_asobu/powerspot/takakukomayama.html 高来神社(高麗神社) ・ 高麗山] (Ōiso Town)</ref>
The shrine was also a center of samurai devotion. [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] is said to have prayed here for the safe childbirth of his wife, [[:en:Hōjō Masako|Hōjō Masako]]. During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the [[:en:Later Hōjō clan|Later Hōjō clan]] donated a small piece of land to the shrine.
The shrine stands in the former village of {{ill|Koma Village|ja|高麗村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma Village|1=Koma Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11673304}}, later incorporated into [[:en:Ōiso|Ōiso Town]].
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja|神皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami-Musubi-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カミムスビ|1=Kamimusubi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3275901}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦穂邇々伎尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] * [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]
== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years

== Timeline == * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]: Claimed founding era * Reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]: Kami-Musubi and Ninigi-no-Mikoto enshrined * 533 AD (Reign of [[:en:Emperor Ankan|Emperor Ankan]]): Empress Jingū and Emperor Ōjin enshrined * 717 AD (Yōrō 1): Shrine becomes a Buddhist temple complex under [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] * 1591 AD (Tenshō 19): Receives a land grant (100 koku) from the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|徳川幕府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 江戸幕府}} * 1868 AD (Meiji 1): Becomes solely a Shinto shrine ([[:en:Shinbutsu bunri|Shinbutsu bunri]]) * 1873 AD (Meiji 6): Ranked as {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} (district shrine) * 1897 AD (Meiji 30): Renamed to '''Takaku Shrine''' after the nearby historical district of {{ill|Takaku-gō|ja|高来郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takaku-gō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * 1907 AD (Meiji 40): Designated as a shrine for imperial offerings
== Annual Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''' (元旦祭): January 1 * '''Setsubun Festival''' (節分祭): February 3 * '''Spring Festival''' (春季festival): Closest weekend to April 18 ** Includes a procession ([[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]) from the upper shrine to the main shrine * '''Summer Grand Festival''' (夏季大祭): Closest Sunday to July 18 ** Known as the "{{ill|Ofune Matsuri|ja|御船祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ofune Matsuri|qq=}}" (Boat Festival) on even-numbered years
== Access == * [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Tōkaidō Main Line|Tōkaidō Main Line (JR East)]]: From [[:en:Ōiso Station|Ōiso Station]], take the bus toward [[:en:Hiratsuka Station|Hiratsuka Station]] North Exit and alight at "Kesho-zaka" or "Hanamizu" bus stop (3 minutes on foot).
